2604.06832.title: Fast-dVLM: Efficient Block-Diffusion VLM via Direct Conversion from Autoregressive VLM
2604.06832.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) predominantly rely on autoregressive decoding, which generates tokens one at a time and fundamentally limits inference throughput. This limitation is especially acute in physical AI scenarios such as robotics and autonomous driving, where VLMs are deployed on edge devices at batch size one, making AR decoding memory-bandwidth-bound and leaving hardware parallelism underutilized. While block-wise discrete diffusion has shown promise for parallel text generation, extending it to VLMs remains challenging due to the need to jointly handle continuous visual representations and discrete text tokens while preserving pretrained multimodal capabilities. We present Fast-dVLM, a block-diffusion-based VLM that enables KV-cache-compatible parallel decoding and speculative block decoding for inference acceleration. We systematically compare two AR-to-diffusion conversion strategies: a two-stage approach that first adapts the LLM backbone with text-only diffusion fine-tuning before multimodal training, and a direct approach that converts the full AR VLM in one stage. Under comparable training budgets, direct conversion proves substantially more efficient by leveraging the already multimodally aligned VLM; we therefore adopt it as our recommended recipe. We introduce a suite of multimodal diffusion adaptations, block size annealing, causal context attention, auto-truncation masking, and vision efficient concatenation, that collectively enable effective block diffusion in the VLM setting. Extensive experiments across 11 multimodal benchmarks show Fast-dVLM matches its autoregressive counterpart in generation quality. With SGLang integration and FP8 quantization, Fast-dVLM achieves over 6x end-to-end inference speedup over the AR baseline.
2604.06832.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）主要依賴自回歸解碼，這會一次生成一個標記，根本上限制了推理吞吐量。這一限制在物理 AI 場景中尤為明顯，例如機器人技術和自動駕駛，其中 VLMs 在邊緣設備上以批量大小為一進行部署，使得自回歸解碼受到內存帶寬的限制，並使硬體並行性未被充分利用。雖然基於區塊的離散擴散在並行文本生成方面顯示出潛力，但將其擴展到 VLMs 仍然具有挑戰性，因為需要共同處理連續的視覺表示和離散的文本標記，同時保持預訓練的多模態能力。我們提出了 Fast-dVLM，一種基於區塊擴散的 VLM，能夠實現 KV-cache 兼容的並行解碼和推理加速的推測性區塊解碼。我們系統地比較了兩種自回歸到擴散的轉換策略：一種是兩階段的方法，首先通過僅文本的擴散微調來適應 LLM 主幹，然後進行多模態訓練；另一種是直接的方法，將完整的自回歸 VLM 在一個階段中轉換。在可比的訓練預算下，直接轉換證明通過利用已經多模態對齊的 VLM 更加高效；因此，我們將其作為推薦的方法。我們介紹了一系列多模態擴散適應技術，包括區塊大小退火、因果上下文注意、自動截斷掩碼和視覺高效串接，這些技術共同實現了在 VLM 設定中的有效區塊擴散。在 11 個多模態基準測試中的廣泛實驗顯示，Fast-dVLM 在生成質量上與其自回歸對應物相匹配。通過 SGLang 集成和 FP8 量化，Fast-dVLM 在端到端推理速度上實現了超過 6 倍的加速，相較於自回歸基準。
2604.06832.author: Chengyue Wu et.al.
2604.06832.authors: Chengyue Wu, Shiyi Lan, Yonggan Fu, Sensen Gao, Jin Wang, Jincheng Yu, Jose M. Alvarez, Pavlo Molchanov, Ping Luo, Song Han, Ligeng Zhu, Enze Xie
